mnemonics: Trusted Hypervisors Are Virtual Security Guards: They Isolate, Verify, and Protect all hotel (cloud) guests (VMs).
keyPoints: A trusted hypervisor is a security-hardened virtual machine monitor (VMM) that provides a secure foundation for virtualization.
Its core function is to enforce strong isolation between virtual machines (VMs) to prevent one VM from accessing the memory or data of another.
It achieves this through mechanisms like a Trusted Computing Base (TCB), measured launch, and attestation to prove its integrity to a remote party.
commonMistakes: Assuming a standard hypervisor is inherently secure and provides the same level of isolation as a trusted one designed for security.
Confusing the hypervisor's role with guest VM security; a compromised trusted hypervisor can undermine all VMs, but secure VMs do not compensate for a weak hypervisor.
Believing that simply using a trusted hypervisor makes the entire cloud platform secure, ignoring the shared responsibility model which includes guest OS and application security.
